purpose: The morphology side of the token state machine. An observed SURFACE (what is written, diacritics and all) is not the same as the HIDDEN morphological state (root + wazn + Form + voice + the certified sense). This file encodes the licensed transitions from an observation to a hidden state, the evidence each transition requires, and the forbidden transitions that past regressions taught us to refuse. A transition with requires_two_vote=true may not be taken on a single rung — it needs two independent agreeing certifiers (e.g. QAC root + Qamus entry, or norm_strict + QAC). norm() is never a vote. Public artifacts stay {src:'qamus', kind:'authored', lang:'en'}; QAC is INTERNAL evidence only.

fields.from_observation: the surface signal observed on the diacritized token
fields.trigger_evidence: the certifier(s) that license moving to the hidden state
fields.to_hidden_state: the morphological state the transition asserts (root/wazn/Form/voice/sense)
fields.requires_two_vote: true if a single rung is insufficient; two independent certifiers must agree
fields.why: for forbidden transitions: the regression this refusal prevents

state_vocabulary.observation: diacritized surface string as written in the muṣḥaf (incl. seat, shadda, harakāt, proclitics)
state_vocabulary.certifiers: qamus_entry | qac_root | photographed_source_page | norm_strict | (norm — assists lookup, NEVER votes)
state_vocabulary.pending: the explicit non-state emitted when evidence is insufficient; safe default

voting_rule: A 'vote' is one INDEPENDENT certifier agreeing on the hidden state. norm() is not a vote (it only assists recall — see norm_never_certifies in ../rules/root-decision-rules.json). Two votes from the SAME source (e.g. two QAC fields) count as one. requires_two_vote transitions are the hamza-sensitive, Form-ambiguous, and voice-ambiguous ones where a single rung has historically shipped a wrong gloss.

transitions.from_observation: stem + sound-plural suffix ـُونَ/ـِينَ or ـَاتٌ/ـَاتٍ
transitions.trigger_evidence: norm_strict strips the case suffix to a lemma; QAC POS confirms a sound plural
transitions.to_hidden_state: {root: lemma-root, wazn: sound-plural, sense: singular's sense, number: plural}

transitions.from_observation: أَفْعَال / مَفَاعِل / فُعَلَاء broken-plural shape with full diacritics
transitions.trigger_evidence: QAC root + the certified singular's Qamus entry agree on the same root
transitions.to_hidden_state: {root: certified, wazn: broken-plural, sense: singular's sense, number: plural}

transitions.trigger_evidence: norm_strict restores the underlying أَأ Form-IV augment AND QAC marks Form IV
QAC marks Form II AND the entry's sense is the intensive/causative reading

transitions.from_observation: ḍamma on the first radical + kasra before the last (فُعِلَ / yufʿalu)
transitions.trigger_evidence: QAC voice=passive AND the context/agreement supports an undergoer subject
norm_strict preserves the seat; QAC certifies one constant root across the inflections
transitions.to_hidden_state: {root: single, sense: single; seat treated as a vowel-context spelling rule}

transitions.from_observation: initial ٱ (hamzat al-waṣl) before a stem
transitions.trigger_evidence: ../rules/hamza-gates.json gate-wasl-strip removes the waṣl; QAC root certifies the residual stem
transitions.to_hidden_state: {root: residual-stem-root; the waṣl is NOT a radical}

transitions.from_observation: any surface where the disambiguating seat/harakah/shadda is ABSENT

forbidden_transitions.pattern: norm_strict key نزل → a single gloss for both نَزَّلَ and نَزَلَ
forbidden_transitions.why: نَزَّلَ (shadda on zāy, Form II) 'sent down (gradually/upon)' and نَزَلَ (Form I) 'descended / came down' share the bare key نزل. Authoring one gloss for both erases the Form II↔I distinction and the agent/patient roles. The shadda is the signal; Form must be certified by QAC + entry before glossing, never by the shared key.

forbidden_transitions.pattern: observation آمَنَ → {sense: 'to be safe'} on a single rung
forbidden_transitions.why: آمَنَ (madda, Form IV) is 'to believe', أَمِنَ (plain hamza, Form I) is 'to be safe' — same root أ م ن, different Form and sense. A single-rung transition that picked the Form-I sense for a Form-IV surface shipped the wrong gloss. Requires_two_vote; see amana-vs-amina-seats in ../rules/hamza-gates.json.

forbidden_transitions.pattern: observation إِيمَان → {root: ي م ن, sense: 'oaths'}
forbidden_transitions.why: إِيمَان is root أ م ن 'faith'; أَيْمَان is root ي م ن 'oaths'. norm() fuses them by dropping the seat. Transitioning the faith-surface to the oaths-state is the canonical hamza-collapse regression — forbidden without the seat-preserving two-vote.

forbidden_transitions.pattern: observation فُعِلَ-shaped surface → {voice: active} by default
forbidden_transitions.why: Defaulting passive-pattern surfaces (قُتِلَ, أُنزِلَ) to active flips agent and patient and reverses the verse's meaning. Voice is a requires_two_vote hidden state: QAC voice + agreement must agree before authoring active OR passive.

forbidden_transitions.pattern: observation with sound-plural suffix → {root includes the suffix}
forbidden_transitions.why: Counting ـُونَ/ـِينَ/ـَات as radicals fabricates a quadriliteral root (مُسْلِمُونَ → false root س ل م ن). The suffix is inflection; strip it via norm_strict first. Mirrors suffix-as-radical in ../rules/plural-gender-rules.json.

Treating hamzat al-waṣl as a radical hamza invents an أ-initial root for Form VII/VIII/X stems and the article (ٱسْتَغْفَرَ → false root أ س ت). The waṣl carries no glottal stop in continuous reading; strip it first (gate-wasl-strip).

forbidden_transitions.pattern: any forbidden/ambiguous observation → a non-pending state via norm() alone
forbidden_transitions.why: norm() assists lookup but is not a vote; letting it complete a transition is the root cause of every collapse above. When in doubt the only licensed target is pending — prefer pending over wrong, per ../rules/root-decision-rules.json decision rules.
